summary: Re-export cleaned corpus to guru-web PG + verify query mis-hits resolved
description:
Make the cleanup actually land in retrieval: re-export + verify. (Depends on C1; re-runnable after each strip child.)

After the strip/drop children, the local corpus + guru.db are clean. Re-export to
the guru-web Postgres (scripts/export.py → VPS load; see memory
project_vps_psql_pattern: `sudo -u postgres psql guru < file.sql`). Then verify
the original query mis-hits are resolved: re-run the queries that previously
surfaced boilerplate and confirm clean primary-source chunks now rank correctly.

This can run incrementally — after C1 alone it already delivers the ~30% win; re-run
after C2-C4 land.

Done contract:
- export.py run; guru-web PG row counts (chunks/embeddings) match the local cleaned corpus.
- Before/after retrieval check on >=1 query that previously mis-hit on boilerplate
  now returns clean content (capture the two outputs).
- Confirm EXPECTED_SCHEMA_VERSION alignment if export touches schema (see
  docs/concept-hierarchy/guru-web-alignment.md).

resolution.note: Prod re-export + verification handled manually by the user (DB exported + verified). Local cleaned corpus/guru.db consistent (C1-C4).
